questions.prompt: What is an atom?
options: The smallest part of an element that can still exist

questions.prompt: What is an element?
options: A substance made of two types of atom bonded together
A substance made of only one type of atom

questions.prompt: What is a compound?
options: A mixture of two elements in any proportion
Two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions

questions.prompt: Which formula represents a compound?

A compound has different properties from the elements it is made from.
Mixtures are chemically combined in fixed proportions, like compounds.

The small, dense, positively charged nucleus

Have no effect on atomic structure

quizzes.title: 6.1 Rates of Reaction: Factors and Collision Theory

questions.prompt: According to collision theory, for a reaction to occur, particles must:
options: Simply be present in the same container
Collide with sufficient energy (activation energy) and the correct orientation

questions.prompt: Increasing temperature increases the rate of reaction because:
options: Particles move faster and collide more often, with more energy

questions.prompt: Increasing concentration (of a solution) increases the rate of reaction because:
options: The volume of the container increases
There are more particles in a given volume, so collisions happen more frequently

questions.prompt: Increasing the surface area of a solid reactant increases the rate of reaction because:
options: More particles are exposed and available to react, increasing collision frequency
Fewer particles are available to react

questions.prompt: Increasing pressure of a gas can increase the rate of reaction, similar to increasing concentration.

What term describes the minimum energy needed for a collision to result in a reaction?

acceptedAnswers: more particles exposed, increasing collision frequency

questions.prompt: Dynamic equilibrium can only be reached in:
